LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS MARK MARROCCO AND HEATHER MARROCCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE PARTIES WAIVED PARAGRAPH 4 [MODIFICATION] OF THEIR AGREEMENT. THE DECLARATION OF JOHN STEUERNAGEL ESTABLISHES THAT THE PARTIES MAY HAVE EXECUTED AN ORAL AGREEMENT THAT INDICATES A WAIVER. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MARK MARROCCO AND HEATHER MARROCCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE WRITTEN CONTRACT ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT A TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ("SAC") STATES THAT THE $28,850 WAS AN ESTIMATE, AND THE PARTIES WOULD BE SUBSEQUENTLY BILLED BI-WEEKLY FOR WORK ACTUALLY PERFORMED. ADDITIONALLY, THE SAC ALLEGES THAT PLAINTIFF PERFORMED "EXTRA WORK PURSUANT TO CHANGE ORDERS." (SAC, PAR. 7.) THIS IS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE WRITTEN CONTRACT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: George Lee, Esq., 415.979.0500, for plaintiff Sculpt Gardens Inc.
Daniel Rockey, Esq., 415.268.1986, for defendants Mark Marrocco and Heather Marrocco.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS MARK MARROCCO AND HEATHER MARROCCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. The written contract attached as Exhibit A to the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") states that the $28,850 was an estimate, and the parties would be subsequently billed bi-weekly for work actually performed. Additionally, the SAC alleges that Plaintiff performed "extra work pursuant to change orders." (SAC, par. 7.) This is sufficient to state a cause of action for breach of the written contract. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj
Off Calendar Apr-16-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GEORGE M. LEE OF THE SEILER EPSTEIN ZIEGLER & APPLEGATE, LLP, TEL. NO. 979-0500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DANIEL ROCKEY OF BRYAN CAVE,LLP, TEL. NO. 268-1986 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs MARK MARROCCO and HEATHER MARROCCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT or in the alternative summary adjudication - The motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the parties waived paragraph 4 [modification] of their agreement. The declaration of John Steuernagel establishes that the parties may have executed an oral agreement that indicates a waiver. The Court denies the motion for summary adjudication. Defendant's notice of motion and separate statement of undisputed facts does not comply with CRC 3.1350. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
